presentation.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future

slides.title: Building My Future — Overview
slides.body:
Your Venture Identity is built from five pillars:

1. Ambition — who you want to become
2. Impact — who you want to serve
3. Values — what you will never compromise
4. Future Self — your 3-year narrative
5. Career Direction — Agency Builder or Specialist Consultant

Each pillar becomes a builder activity in Venture Blueprint™ and a section in your Venture Portfolio™.
slides.speakerNotes:
The greatest entrepreneurs never succeed alone.
Pause.
They have mentors.
Pause.
They have partners.
Pause.
They have teammates.
Pause.
They have technology that helps them think better.
Pause.
SPIKE gives you all four.
discussionQuestions: Which pillar feels hardest to articulate right now?
Why does identity come before market research?

slides.title: Ambition
slides.body:
Ambition is not a wish list — it is a declaration of who you are becoming.

Strong ambition statements are:
• Specific (role + outcome)
• Personal (your words, not copied phrases)
• Entrepreneurial (builder mindset, not employee mindset)

Example: 'Become a respected Agency Director who develops leaders and builds a thriving organization.'

You will craft yours with Venture Coach™ — guided conversation, not blank forms.
slides.speakerNotes:
One thing you'll notice throughout SPIKE is that we don't use AI to think for you.
(Pause.)
We use AI to help you think better.
Continue
The ideas...
The dreams...
The purpose...
The mission...
The venture...
They all come from you.
(Pause.)
AI simply helps you sharpen them.
Point to the Laptop
Imagine you're writing your Purpose Statement.
You already know what you want to say.
But maybe you're not sure if it's inspiring enough.
Or clear enough.
Or specific enough.
Point to the AI Suggestions
Instead of giving you the answer...
SPIKE asks better questions.
"Can you make this clearer?"
"Can you compare different approaches?"
"Can you strengthen your impact?"
"Can you think bigger?"
The Philosophy
That's how entrepreneurs grow.
Not because somebody gives them answers...
but because somebody asks better questions.
(Pause.)
In SPIKE...
AI becomes that thinking partner.
Connect to Mentors
And that's exactly what your mentors will do as well.
The AI helps you think.
Your squad helps you explore.
Your mentors help you challenge your assumptions.
Together, they make your ideas stronger.
Bring it Back to the Audience
By the end of this week...
your Venture Blueprint won't be something AI created.
(Pause.)
It will be something you created—
refined by AI,
strengthened by your squad,
challenged by your mentor,
and inspired by your own purpose.
Transition
So let's begin with the very first question every entrepreneur must answer.
(Pause.)
Why do you want to build this venture?
Then transition directly into the Venture Blueprint live demonstration
discussionQuestions: Say your ambition aloud in one sentence — even if it is imperfect.
What makes an ambition statement feel authentic vs performative?

slides.title: Impact
slides.body:
Impact answers: Who do you want to help — and how will their lives be different because of you?

Impact connects AIA's protection mission to your personal venture.

Strong impact statements name:
• A specific audience (professionals, families, young parents, freelancers)
• A transformation (peace of mind, financial clarity, protected legacy)

Your impact feeds Business Plan Chapter 1 and your portfolio Identity section.
slides.speakerNotes: We will be using this tool.
discussionQuestions: Who is one person you already want to help — by name or by type?
What problem do they face that financial planning could solve?

slides.title: Values
slides.body:
Values are your non-negotiables — the principles that guide decisions when pressure is high.

In SPIKE you will:
• Select values that resonate
• Rank your top three
• See how they shape your venture culture

Values are not decoration. They appear in your squad charter, mentor conversations, and Hour 200 Venture Board presentation.
discussionQuestions: Which value would you never compromise — even if it cost you a sale?
How will your squad know what you stand for?

slides.title: Future Self
slides.body:
Future Self is your 3-year narrative — a story of who you have become.

Not a job description. A life picture:
• Professional identity
• Lifestyle and financial milestones
• Relationships and team
• Legacy and contribution

Venture Coach™ helps you build this through an interactive timeline. Your Future Self anchors every Week 1 conversation with your mentor.
slides.speakerNotes:
Speaker: This will be your Venture Blueprint.
"By the end of today, every one of you will have your own Venture Blueprint.”
discussionQuestions: Describe yourself three years from now — present tense, as if it already happened.
What daily habit today makes that future self possible?

slides.title: Career Direction
slides.body:
SPIKE offers two venture paths — exploration today, commitment by Day 5:

Agency Builder — build a team, recruit, scale an organization.
Specialist Consultant — build deep expertise and a premium practice.

No wrong answer on Day 1. Honest exploration beats premature commitment.

Your direction feeds Career Track in Venture Portfolio and canvas engines in Week 1.
slides.speakerNotes: Today, we're going to build all of these.
discussionQuestions: Which path energizes you today — and what evidence supports that?
What would you need to learn before committing to either path?

slides.title: Deck 02 Close — Day 1 Deliverables
slides.body:
By end of Day 1 you will have:

✓ Ambition, Impact, Values, Future Self, Career Direction
✓ Dream Board visual collage
✓ Squad membership and signed charter
✓ Portfolio Identity section started
✓ Mentor snapshot for coaching

Program Coaches and mentors use Day 1 evaluation templates to observe — not grade — your engagement and clarity.
slides.speakerNotes:
Your Purpose
"You've just refined your ambition."
(Pause.)
"Now we're going one level deeper."
"Ambition answers the question: What do I want to achieve?"
(Pause.)
"Purpose answers a different question."
(Pause.)
"Why does this venture deserve to exist?"
"A successful venture isn't built around a product."
"It begins with a problem worth solving and people worth serving."
"Don't worry about writing the perfect purpose statement today."
"We're still building Version 1."
"Think about someone whose life becomes better because your venture exists."
(Pause.)
"If your venture disappeared tomorrow..."
"Who would miss it?"
(Pause.)
"And why?"
"Open your Venture Blueprint."
"Navigate to Purpose."
"You have twenty minutes."
"Start building.
discussionQuestions: What is the one deliverable you will finish before you leave today?
What will you ask your mentor at first check-in?

slides.speakerNotes:
Instead of giving them answers, walk around the room asking questions like:
"Who are you trying to help?"
"What frustrates them today?"
"Why does this matter to you personally?"
"If you could solve only one problem, what would it be?"
"Why should anyone care that your venture exists?"
Transition to the Next Slide (Share)
As the timer ends:
"Your first draft doesn't have to be perfect."
"Now let's see how your Squad can make it stronger."
"Turn to your Squad."

Prompt
Imagine your life five years from now.
Where are you?
Who are you helping?
What does success look like?
Large inspirational image.
20 minutes.
